items.title: Confirm the beta installer release checklist
items.agent_suggestion: Open the latest release checklist and confirm the remaining blockers before cutting the installer.
items.note_text: Make sure the packaged desktop shell, bundled local service, and seeded workspace all launch cleanly on a fresh machine.
items.prerequisite: Verify the setup.exe output and confirm the seeded dashboard surfaces are visible after first launch.

items.title: Polish the first-launch notes and mirror copy
items.agent_suggestion: Reuse the beta mirror digest so notes, dashboard, and mirror all tell the same first-run story.
items.note_text: The first launch should feel intentionally populated rather than demo-empty. Keep the copy calm, useful, and release-oriented.
items.prerequisite: Mirror references and task summaries need to point at the same generated workspace documents.

items.title: Collect beta feedback into the next triage round
items.agent_suggestion: Keep the first feedback batch focused on install success, launch health, and seed quality before wider feature requests.
items.note_text: After the beta release lands, collect the first round of install and startup feedback before broadening the roadmap.
items.prerequisite: Wait for at least one full install cycle from a fresh machine and one upgrade rehearsal.

items.title: Review the beta installer metrics every Monday
items.agent_suggestion: Use the same review template each Monday so installer regressions are easy to compare across beta drops.
items.note_text: Each Monday, scan installer success, launch health, notes visibility, mirror summary quality, and settings defaults before planning the next beta cut.

items.title: Archive the beta packaging dry run notes
items.agent_suggestion: Keep the dry run notes nearby so regression reports can reference the original baseline.
items.note_text: The first dry run is already complete and archived for future comparisons.
